name: Opening
purpose: Establish tone, world and point of view in the first minutes.
typicalRisk: A flat opening that states the world instead of dramatising it.

name: Setup
purpose: Plant the characters, wants and rules the story will pay off later.
typicalRisk: Exposition dumps that stall momentum before anything happens.

name: Inciting incident
purpose: Break the status quo and force the protagonist into the story.
typicalRisk: The disruption lands too softly to commit the hero to action.

name: Confrontation
purpose: Put opposing wants in direct collision and raise the stakes.
typicalRisk: Conflict that is talked about rather than played out.

name: Midpoint
purpose: Shift the goal or the ground rules so the second half feels different.
typicalRisk: A sagging middle where nothing meaningfully changes.

name: Reveal
purpose: Deliver new information that recolours everything before it.
typicalRisk: A twist that feels arbitrary because it was never set up.

name: Chase
purpose: Drive the story forward with physical, time-pressured pursuit.
typicalRisk: Spectacle with no stakes the audience actually cares about.

name: Intimacy
purpose: Slow down for closeness, vulnerability and quiet character truth.
typicalRisk: Sentimentality that tips the scene into melodrama.

name: Reversal
purpose: Flip fortunes so the path to the goal suddenly closes or opens.
typicalRisk: A turn so abrupt the audience loses the thread of cause and effect.

name: Climax
purpose: Force the final, decisive confrontation of the central conflict.
typicalRisk: An anticlimax that resolves the plot without testing the theme.

name: Aftermath
purpose: Show the new normal and let the change land emotionally.
typicalRisk: Overstaying the ending and draining the climax of its power.
